info.name: LexBuild Data API
info.description:
Complete collection for the LexBuild Data API — programmatic access to the U.S. Code, eCFR (Code of Federal Regulations), and Federal Register.

**Base URL**: `https://lexbuild.dev/api` (production) or `http://localhost:4322/api` (local dev)

**Authentication**: Pass your API key via `X-API-Key` header, `Authorization: Bearer <key>` header, or `?api_key=<key>` query parameter. Anonymous access is available at 100 requests/minute.

**Documentation**: https://lexbuild.dev/docs/api

**Content Negotiation**: Single-document endpoints support `?format=json|markdown|text` or the `Accept` header (`text/markdown`, `text/plain`).

**Rate Limits**:
- Anonymous: 100 req/min
- Standard: 1,000 req/min
- Elevated: 5,000 req/min
- Unlimited: No limit

Rate limit headers (`X-RateLimit-Limit`, `X-RateLimit-Remaining`, `X-RateLimit-Reset`, `X-RateLimit-Policy`) are included on every response.

**Identifier Variables**: Keep raw human-readable identifiers in the environment (`t1/s1`, `t17/s240.10b-5`, `2026-06029`). The collection pre-request script derives canonical and URL-safe path variables for single-document requests, so USC, eCFR, and FR identifiers do not need to be stored pre-encoded.

**Saved Examples**: Embedded response examples are fixture-backed samples that illustrate response shape and representative values rather than corpus scale.

item.name: System
item.description: Health checks, source metadata, corpus statistics, and OpenAPI specification.

request.description: Returns API health status, database connectivity, document count, and uptime.
Returns metadata for all available legal sources, including filterable/sortable fields, hierarchy structure, and document counts.

response.name: Sources
response.body:
{
  "data": [
    {
      "id": "usc",
      "name": "United States Code",
      "short_name": "USC",
      "description": "General and permanent federal statutes organized into 54 titles.",
      "url_prefix": "/usc",
      "hierarchy": [
        "title",
        "chapter",
        "section"
      ],
      "filterable_fields": [
        "title_number",
        "chapter_number",
        "status",
        "positive_law",
        "legal_status"
      ],
      "sortable_fields": [
        "title_number",
        "section_number",
        "identifier",
        "last_updated"
      ],
      "has_titles": true,
      "document_count": 4
    },
    {
      "id": "ecfr",
      "name": "eCFR",
      "short_name": "eCFR",
      "description": "Electronic Code of Federal Regulations, updated continuously by the Government Publishing Office.",
      "url_prefix": "/ecfr",
      "hierarchy": [
        "title",
        "chapter",
        "part",
        "section"
      ],
      "filterable_fields": [
        "title_number",
        "chapter_number",
        "part_number",
        "agency",
        "status",
        "legal_status"
      ],
      "sortable_fields": [
        "title_number",
        "section_number",
        "identifier",
        "last_updated"
      ],
      "has_titles": true,
      "document_count": 3
    },
    {
      "id": "fr",
      "name": "Federal Register",
      "short_name": "FR",
      "description": "Daily journal of the U.S. government: rules, proposed rules, notices, and presidential documents.",
      "url_prefix": "/fr",
      "hierarchy": [
        "year",
        "month",
        "document"
      ],
      "filterable_fields": [
        "document_type",
        "agency",
        "publication_date",
        "effective_date"
      ],
      "sortable_fields": [
        "publication_date",
        "document_number",
        "identifier"
      ],
      "has_titles": false,
      "document_count": 4
    }
  ],
  "meta": {
    "api_version": "v1",
    "timestamp": "2026-04-11T00:00:00.000Z"
  }
}

request.description: Returns aggregate statistics for the entire corpus: total documents, per-source breakdowns, title counts, date ranges, and document type distributions.
Returns the auto-generated OpenAPI 3.1 specification document describing all API endpoints, schemas, and parameters.

item.name: Search
item.description: Full-text search across all sources powered by Meilisearch. Supports filtering by source, document type, agency, date range, and custom facets.

request.description: Search across all sources with default settings (20 results, relevance sort, highlights enabled, source+status facets).

response.name: Search Results

query.description: Filter to a single source: usc, ecfr, or fr

request.description: Search within a specific source (`usc`, `ecfr`, or `fr`) by editing `{{search_source}}` in the environment.
Search with multiple FR-specific filters combined: source, document type, publication date range, and optional agency.

query.description: Sort: relevance, publication_date, -publication_date, title_number, identifier, document_number

request.description: Paginated search with custom limit and sort order.

query.description: Comma-separated: source, title_number, document_type, agency, status, granularity, publication_date

request.description: Search with custom facet distribution fields. Edit `{{search_facets}}` in the environment to try different allowed facet combinations.
Search with highlighted snippets disabled for cleaner programmatic consumption.

item.description: Endpoints for the United States Code — 54 titles of general and permanent federal statutes.

request.description: List USC documents with default pagination (20 results, sorted by identifier).

request.description: List documents from a specific USC title.

query.description: Filter by status: current, repealed, reserved, transferred

request.description: List current positive-law sections, sorted by title number.

query.description: Return metadata only, no body content

request.description: List documents returning only metadata (no body text). Useful for building indexes or catalogs.

query.description: Keyset pagination cursor — use the last document's sort key from the previous page

request.description: Paginate using keyset/cursor pagination for stable page boundaries. Use the sort key of the last document from the previous response as the cursor value. Cursor-paginated responses return `pagination.total` as `null` and provide the next cursor in `pagination.next`.

item.name: Single Document
item.description: Retrieve a single USC document by identifier. Keep `{{usc_identifier}}` human-readable in the environment; the collection pre-request script canonicalizes and URL-encodes the path value automatically.

request.description: Get a USC document by identifier. Set `{{usc_identifier}}` to shorthand (`t1/s1`) or a canonical identifier (`/us/usc/t1/s1`); the collection pre-request script canonicalizes and URL-encodes the path value automatically. Returns JSON with metadata and body by default.

response.name: Document (JSON)
response.body:
{
  "data": {
    "id": "us-usc-t1-s1",

      "source": "usc",
      "legal_status": "law",

}

query.description: Return raw Markdown with YAML frontmatter

request.description: Get the document as raw Markdown with YAML frontmatter. Set `{{usc_identifier}}` to a shorthand or canonical identifier; the collection pre-request script handles the path encoding. Content-Type: text/markdown.

query.description: Return plain text with Markdown syntax stripped

request.description: Get the document as plain text (Markdown syntax stripped). Set `{{usc_identifier}}` to a shorthand or canonical identifier; the collection pre-request script handles the path encoding. Content-Type: text/plain.
Get document metadata without the body text. Set `{{usc_identifier}}` to a shorthand or canonical identifier; the collection pre-request script handles the path encoding.

exec: pm.test('Returns USC metadata without body content', function () {

query.description: Comma-separated field names. 'identifier' and 'source' are always included.

request.description: Get only specific fields. The `identifier` and `source` fields are always included. Include `body` explicitly to get the document text. The collection pre-request script canonicalizes and URL-encodes `{{usc_identifier}}` before the request is sent.

header.description: Request Markdown format via Accept header

request.description: Get the document as Markdown using the Accept header instead of the format query parameter. The collection pre-request script canonicalizes and URL-encodes `{{usc_identifier}}` before the request is sent.

item.name: eCFR

request.description: List eCFR documents with default pagination (20 results, sorted by identifier).
List documents from a specific CFR title.
List eCFR documents from a specific regulatory agency.
List eCFR documents within a specific CFR part (e.g., Part 240 — Securities Exchange Act rules).

query.description: Return metadata only, no body content

request.description: List documents returning only metadata (no body text).

item.name: Single Document
item.description: Retrieve a single eCFR document by identifier. Keep `{{ecfr_identifier}}` human-readable in the environment; the collection pre-request script canonicalizes and URL-encodes the path value automatically.

request.description: Get an eCFR document by identifier. Set `{{ecfr_identifier}}` to shorthand (`t17/s240.10b-5`) or a canonical identifier (`/us/cfr/t17/s240.10b-5`); the collection pre-request script canonicalizes and URL-encodes the path value automatically. Metadata includes eCFR-specific fields: authority, regulatory_source, agency, cfr_part, cfr_subpart.

item.name: Federal Register
item.description: Endpoints for the Federal Register — daily journal of the U.S. Government containing rules, proposed rules, notices, and presidential documents.

item.name: Documents
item.description: List and filter Federal Register documents.

request.description: List FR documents with default pagination (20 results, sorted newest first).
List Federal Register documents filtered by document type.
List FR documents published within a date range.
List Federal Register documents from a specific agency.
List FR documents by effective date. Useful for finding rules that take effect in a given period.
List documents returning only metadata (no body text).

item.name: Single Document
item.description: Retrieve a single Federal Register document by identifier. Keep `{{fr_identifier}}` as a document number or canonical identifier in the environment; the collection pre-request script canonicalizes and URL-encodes the path value automatically.

request.description: Get an FR document by identifier. Set `{{fr_identifier}}` to a document number (`2026-06029`) or canonical identifier (`/us/fr/2026-06029`); the collection pre-request script canonicalizes and URL-encodes the path value automatically. Metadata includes FR-specific fields: document_number, document_type, publication_date, agencies, fr_citation, fr_volume, effective_date, comments_close_date, fr_action, docket_ids, cfr_references.

response.name: Document (JSON)
response.body:
